data.title: Russia considers extending export quotas for some fertilisers, TASS reports
data.description:
Russia's government is considering extending export quotas for certain fertilisers, Russia's state news agency TASS reported, citing Deputy Prime Minister Dmitry Patrushev.

Patrushev did not specify which types of fertilisers could be subject to the quota extension.

data.title: RKLB, LUNR, FLY – Analyst Sees Upside For These Stocks As Lunar Boom Drives Fresh Optimism For Space Players
data.description:
Key points:
  • The analyst noted that FireFly Aerospace’s SciTec acquisition could enhance its foothold in defense-focused space technology.
  • The Lanteris deal is expected to expand Intuitive Machines into a broader, end-to-end, and national-security services provider, according to Roth Capital. 
  • The firm said Rocket Lab’s Electron program entered 2026 strong, with about 20% growth in launches expected.

Roth Capital has turned more bullish on space-focused companies, including Firefly Aerospace Inc. (FLY), Intuitive Machines Inc. (LUNR), and Rocket Lab Corp. (RKLB), citing strong demand trends tied to lunar exploration and defense spending.

Analyst Suji Desilva increased the price target for FireFly Aerospace to $60 from $35, maintaining a ‘Buy’ rating, according to TheFly. The firm pointed to expanding opportunities driven by government initiatives and recent acquisitions.

Firefly Aerospace’s Growth Potential

The analyst emphasized the company’s progress in scaling its launch and spacecraft operations. FireFly’s acquisition of SciTec is expected to strengthen its position in defense-related space technology, aligning with increases in government spending.

A key catalyst, according to Roth, is NASA’s plan to ramp up lunar cargo missions, potentially reaching a monthly cadence in the coming years.

Firefly Aerospace stock inched over 0.3% in Friday’s premarket. On Stocktwits, retail sentiment around the stock jumped to ‘bullish’ from ‘bearish’ territory the previous day. Message volume turned to ‘high’ from ‘low’ levels in 24 hours.

Intuitive Machines Positioned For Expansion

Roth also boosted its price target on Intuitive Machines to $35 from $25, implying a 28% upside potential to the stock’s closing price on Thursday. The firm sees the company as well placed to benefit from rising investment in lunar exploration.

Intuitive Machines’ recent Lanteris acquisition is expected to enhance operational capabilities while unlocking efficiencies. Analysts believe the combined entity can broaden its offerings beyond civil and lunar missions to include national security programs, expanding into end-to-end services.

Intuitive Machines' stock traded 0.8% higher in Friday’s premarket.

Roth raised its price target on Rocket Lab USA Inc. (RKLB) to $100 from $90. The firm pointed to continued traction in both launch services and space systems.

The company’s Electron rocket program has begun 2026 on solid footing, with projections for roughly 20% annual launch growth, the firm said. Rising defense budgets and demand for space infrastructure are expected to further support long-term expansion.

Rocket Lab USA stock has gained over 18% year-to-date.

data.title: State Street Profit Rises On Fee Growth
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller

State Street reported higher first-quarter profit, boosted by fee growth, particularly for its foreign exchange trading services.

The company posted net income of $764 million, or $2.49 a share, up from $644 million, or $2.04 a share.

Adjusted earnings excluding notable items were $2.84 a share. Analysts polled by FactSet expected $2.64 a share.

Revenue rose 16% to $3.8 billion. Wall Street expected $3.67 billion. Total fee revenue increased 15% to $2.96 billion, while net interest income rose 17% to $835 million.

Servicing fees increased 11% from the prior year, management fees rose 23% and foreign exchange trading services fees grew 29%.

Total expenses increased 15%, including repositioning charges, higher revenue-related costs, continued investments and the negative impact of currency translation.

"In a dynamic operating environment, the momentum across investment services, investment management, and markets underscores the strength of our franchise," said Chief Executive Ron O'Hanley.

Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com.

data.title: Viking to Combine With NorthStar in $300 Million SPAC Deal, Add $30 Million PIPE
data.description:
Viking Acquisition Corp I agreed to merge with NorthStar Earth and Space in a $300 million SPAC transaction that will issue 30 million New Viking shares at closing and includes up to 10 million earnout shares tied to future revenue milestones. To support the transaction, Viking secured a $30 million PIPE with investor warrants and a sponsor transfer of 3 million founder shares to PIPE investors. Related agreements include a sponsor support letter, voting and support commitments from key NorthStar holders, post-closing registration rights, and 180-day lock-ups for major stakeholders. The combined company expects to list on the New York Stock Exchange.

Agreement 1: Viking to Combine With NorthStar in $300 Million SPAC Deal, Issue 30 Million Shares

  • Agreement type: Business Combination Agreement (SPAC merger)
  • Counterparty: NorthStar Earth and Space
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 16 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: N/A
  • Reason: Take NorthStar public and fund growth

Agreement 2: Viking Sponsor Backs Merger, To Transfer 3M Founder Shares; Receives 500K New Shares

  • Agreement type: Sponsor Letter Agreement
  • Counterparty: Viking Acquisition Sponsor I
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 16 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: N/A
  • Reason: Secure sponsor support and facilitate financing

Agreement 3: NorthStar Holders Sign Voting Pact Supporting Plan of Arrangement

  • Agreement type: Voting and Support Agreement
  • Counterparty: Certain NorthStar securityholders
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 16 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: N/A
  • Reason: Ensure shareholder approval for the transaction

Agreement 4: New Viking to Grant Registration Rights to Sponsor and Key Holders Post-Closing

  • Agreement type: Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement
  • Counterparty: Sponsor and certain NorthStar securityholders
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 16 2026 / N/A
  • Duration / Termination: N/A
  • Reason: Provide liquidity via resale registration

Agreement 5: New Viking to Implement 180-Day Lock-Ups for Key Holders; Sponsor Lock-Up Set at Six Months

  • Agreement type: Lock-Up Agreements
  • Counterparty: Certain NorthStar securityholders and sponsor
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 16 2026 / N/A
  • Duration / Termination: 180 days (holders); six months (sponsor)
  • Reason: Stabilize trading after the merger

Agreement 6: Viking and NorthStar Arrange $30 Million PIPE With 3M Warrants; Sponsor Share Transfer

  • Agreement type: Securities Purchase Agreements (PIPE financing with warrants)
  • Counterparty: Institutional investors
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 16 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: N/A
  • Reason: Provide capital to support business combination and growth

Original SEC Filing: Viking Acquisition Corp I [ VACI ] - 8-K - Apr. 17, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Britain has a weak hand in Palantir test case
data.description:
By Jennifer Johnson

Campaigners want the country’s health service to ditch a $450 mln contract with the US software group. If the UK had a blank sheet of paper, it wouldn’t allow a foreign firm such influence over its data. But a lack of policy foresight has left Britain with no clear plan B.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow Jennifer Johnson on Bluesky and LinkedIn.

CONTEXT NEWS

UK lawmakers held a debate on April 16 about the rollout of the NHS England’s Federated Data Platform (FDP), a cloud-based IT system provided by U.S. software group Palantir Technologies. The discussion follows a period of scrutiny by civil society groups, including Foxglove and Amnesty International UK, over Palantir’s links to surveillance, policing and military operations.

Campaigners say the FDP could make sensitive NHS data easier to combine, access and repurpose in ways patients cannot easily see or control. They also argue Palantir is an unsuitable partner because of its work with U.S. immigration authorities and Israel’s armed forces – and have urged the government to trigger a February 2027 break clause in the contract.

Palantir has said the NHS retains full ownership of its data and any analysis derived from it. The group’s UK executive vice-chair, Louis Mosley, recently told the BBC's Politics Live programme that it has “no interest in patient data in the UK”.

data.title: Scope for more inflows to safe haven Britain
data.description:
Key points:
  • STOXX 600 flat
  • Luxury, tech higher, energy weak
  • Alstom slumps after trading update
  • Wall St futures rise

SCOPE FOR MORE INFLOWS TO SAFE HAVEN BRITAIN

Britain's FTSE 100 has outperformed both Europe's STOXX 600 and the S&P 500 this year, and State Street says positioning is light enough that more inflows could follow.

The British blue chip benchmark CURRENCYCOM:UK100 is up 6% since the start of January, more than the STOXX 600 - 4% - and the S&P 500 - 2%.

And even at the height of late March's selling, the FTSE only dipped briefly into negative territory for the year, while European and U.S. peers spent weeks under water.

Marija Veitmane, head of equity research at State Street Global Markets, says that due to its significant weighting in energy and other defensive segments, the British market emerged as a "relative safe haven".

What's more, she says, State Street's data "shows that despite the recent rally and sustained inflows, institutional investors' holdings remain below benchmark levels, and they continue to buy."

"This points to a supportive near-term outlook for UK equities. As long as demand for energy exposure remains strong and outflows from Europe and EM persist, the UK market appears well positioned to continue attracting capital and continue its position as a safe haven amongst continuously volatile markets."

(Alun John )

*****

data.title: India bonds slip as oil risk, truce doubts weigh
data.description:
By Khushi Malhotra

Indian government bonds fell on Friday, ending the week flat with U.S.-Iran truce talks still uncertain and the blocked Strait of Hormuz keeping oil prices elevated.

The prospects for peace talks between the U.S. and Iran fluctuated this week, and benchmark Brent crude oil futures stayed above $90 a barrel, keeping traders on edge. On Friday, President Donald Trump said a deal to end the war in Iran could be reached soon, without specifying timing.

"A lot depends on what is the outcome of the proposed ceasefire talks, once the existing one ends on April 21/22 and on the flow of ships through the Strait," said Alok Sharma, head of treasury at ICBC, Mumbai.

India's benchmark 6.48% 2035 bond yield (IN064835G=CC) settled at 6.9049%, versus 6.8884% on Thursday. It was little changed compared to last week's close of 6.9119%, when it logged the biggest drop since October 2019.

Bond yields move inversely to prices.

The benchmark 10-year bond yield is likely to trade between 6.75-6.95% in the near term, with upward bias, analysts at Kotak Mahindra Bank wrote in a note.

The central bank's foreign exchange restrictions to support the rupee have also dulled the appeal of Indian bonds by making it harder and more expensive to hedge exposure. Overseas investors hae sold debt worth $2.26 billion since the war began on February 28.

Separately, New Delhi sold 320 billion rupees worth of 5-year and 40-year notes earlier in the day, but demand remained muted after the auction.

RATES

India's overnight index swap rates were flat-to-higher as U.S. and domestic yields rose.

The one-year OIS rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was up about one basis point at 5.8075%, while the two-year swap rate (INR2YMIBROIS=CC) was steady at 6.01%. The liquid five-year (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) rate rose 2.5 bps to 6.3925%.

($1 = 92.9860 Indian rupees)

data.title: CRML Stock Gets Retail Buzzing After Key Tanbreez Updates – Critical Metals Jumps 7% Pre-Market
data.description:
Key points:
  • Bloomberg reported that the company acquired a 50.5% interest in Tanbreez from Rimbal, bringing its total stake to 92.5%.
  • Tanbreez contains steady rare-earth grades along with the presence of key elements such as gallium, hafnium, cerium, and yttrium
  • Texas Capital initiated coverage of Critical Metals with a ‘Buy’ rating and $20 price target, according to The Fly.

Critical Metals Corp. (CRML) drew strong investor attention on Friday after Greenland reportedly approved the indirect transfer of the mining licence for the highly strategic Tanbreez rare earths project, clearing a major regulatory hurdle for the company’s expansion plans.

The approval follows Critical Metals’ move last October to reportedly increase its stake in Tanbreez Mining, giving it near-total control of the project located in southern Greenland. On Friday, Bloomberg reported that the company acquired a 50.5% interest in Tanbreez from Rimbal, bringing its total stake to 92.5%. The company had not issued an official confirmation at the time of writing.

CRML stock jumped nearly 7% in pre-market trading on Friday, adding to a 4% gain a session earlier. It was also among the top trending tickers on Stocktwits at the time of writing.

Why Tanbreez Matters

The Tanbreez project is home to one of the world’s largest known rare earth deposits and benefits from direct access to North Atlantic shipping routes. Recent drilling by Critical Metals confirmed steady rare-earth grades along with the presence of key elements such as gallium, hafnium, cerium, and yttrium.

The scale and strategic value of these resources have also drawn geopolitical attention. U.S. President Donald Trump previously highlighted Greenland’s importance, noting that securing such assets could give the U.S. greater access to critical minerals and help counter China’s dominance in the rare-earth supply chain.

According to a Reuters report last October, the Trump administration discussed taking a stake in Critical Metals, a move that would give Washington a direct interest in the Tanbreez project.

Texas Capital Bullish On Tanbreez’s Strategic Location, Resource Quality

On Wednesday, Texas Capital initiated coverage of Critical Metals with a ‘Buy’ rating and $20 price target, implying a potential 126% upside to the closing price of $8.89, according to The Fly.

The firm added that the Tanbreez asset could become a key part of the Western rare earth supply chain, citing its significant long-term scale, high-quality resources, and strategic location that supports both U.S. and European markets.

How Did Retail Traders React?

Retail sentiment on Stocktwits remained in the ‘extremely bullish’ zone over the past 24 hours, amid ‘high’ message volumes.

One user said the “door is opening very wide now for the U.S. to invest in CRML.”

https://stocktwits.com/DanaDecoy/message/650560989

The stock has gained around 25% so far this year.

data.title: Pound steadies near pre-war levels despite UK political pressure
data.description:
The British pound remained largely stable on Friday, returning to levels seen before the onset of the Iran war, as currency markets showed limited reaction to renewed political pressure on Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

Sterling held steady against the dollar at $1.35305, reflecting broader rangebound conditions across global currency markets.

Traders appeared to overlook calls for Starmer’s resignation, even as political tensions resurfaced following a security-related controversy involving a senior appointment.

Starmer is facing criticism despite dismissing a senior official after it emerged that Britain’s former ambassador to the United States had failed security vetting but was still appointed.

Markets downplay resignation calls

Market participants showed little concern over the political developments, suggesting that investors do not expect immediate leadership changes.

Against the euro, the pound edged slightly lower, slipping 0.1% to 87.155.

Middle East conflict shifts rate expectations

The ongoing conflict in the Middle East has driven a surge in global energy prices, raising concerns about inflation and economic growth.

This has significantly altered market expectations around UK monetary policy.

Money markets are now pricing in at least one 25 basis point interest rate hike from the Bank of England this year.

This marks a sharp reversal from earlier expectations of two rate cuts before the conflict began.

On Thursday, Andrew Bailey told BBC News that the central bank was “not going to rush to judgements” on interest rate changes.

The pound declined 1.9% in March as the Middle East conflict unsettled markets.

The closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a key route for global energy supplies, added further pressure on the UK’s economic outlook.

Data and sentiment offer support

Despite these challenges, sterling has shown resilience.

It is currently on track for its strongest monthly performance in a year, rising 2.6% in April so far, as investors increasingly anticipate a potential resolution to the conflict.

Additional support came from stronger-than-expected economic data.

The latest GDP figures released by the UK Office for National Statistics exceeded economists’ forecasts, helping to bolster confidence in the British economy.

Meanwhile, Britain also faced the sharpest downgrade in growth forecasts among major advanced economies by the International Monetary Fund earlier this week.

Finance minister Rachel Reeves criticised United States strategy in the conflict, calling it “folly.” Mixed signals from corporate sectors

Corporate updates painted a mixed picture across sectors.

In the consumer space, Tesco said its profit outlook remains uncertain due to ongoing tensions in the Middle East, although its shares still rose.

Healthcare stocks provided some support to the broader market.

Overall, while geopolitical risks and political uncertainty persist, the pound appears to be stabilising as markets focus more on monetary policy signals and economic data rather than domestic political developments.

data.title: Markets Wait For Peace Deal
data.description:
CANBERA (dpa-AFX) - Market sentiment across regions and asset classes remains tethered to expectations of a peace deal to end the Middle East war. Positive earnings updates from the corporate sector also swayed sentiment.

Wall Street Futures are trading with mild gains. Benchmarks in Europe are trading on a mostly positive note. Earlier in the day, Asian markets had finished trading on a negative note.

The Dollar Index has edged down. Sovereign bond yields mostly eased. However, in Japan, it hardened.

Both the crude oil benchmarks have shed around 3 percent amidst optimism about a lasting U.S.-Iran ceasefire. Gold has edged down from the flatline. Cryptocurrencies are trading on a positive note.

data.title: ZAWYA: Ajmal Dubai debuts in China at Notes Shanghai, presenting signature fragrance portfolio
data.description:
Dubai, United Arab Emirates,  Ajmal Dubai, a globally renowned fragrance house from Dubai, has officially marked its entry into the Chinese market through its participation at the prestigious Notes Shanghai exhibition, a premier international platform for the fragrance industry. At the event, the brand introduced a curated selection of its signature collections, reflecting the richness of Middle Eastern perfumery blended with contemporary olfactory artistry.

As one of Asia’s leading exhibitions dedicated to niche fragrances, Notes Shanghai provided Ajmal Dubai with a unique opportunity to engage with perfume enthusiasts, industry professionals, distributors, and retailers within China’s rapidly growing luxury fragrance landscape.

Among the highlights of the showcase were several of Ajmal’s exquisite fragrance collections — Signature Series, Aurum, and Boudoir, the creations under these collections includes Eau D Oud, Amber Wood, Musk series, Aurum Summer, Rose Organza, Whispering Love, Musk Sensuel, Long Island Sunset, and Crimson Santal.

The Musk series under the signature collection, one of the core pillars of Ajmal’s perfumery expertise, attracted significant attention at the exhibition. Featuring variants such as Cashmere Musk, Violet Musk, and Amber Musk, the collection presents unique interpretations of musk, ranging from soft floral nuances to warm, amber-infused depth.

In addition, Ajmal presented other signature fragrances that demonstrate the brand’s ability to blend traditional Arabian ingredients with evolving global preferences, highlighting its craftsmanship and versatility in fragrance creation.

Commenting on the brand’s participation, Abdulla Ajmal, CEO of Ajmal Dubai, said, “Our presence at Notes Shanghai marks an important milestone in our global journey. China is a vibrant and dynamic market for niche fragrances, and we are proud to introduce the rich heritage of Arabian perfumery to this discerning audience. Through our diverse portfolio, we aim to showcase our legacy in the art of fragrance creation while building meaningful connections with fragrance lovers and partners in the region.”.

About Ajmal Dubai

Rooted in the rich heritage of Arabian perfumery, Ajmal Perfumes began its perfumery journey with the world’s oldest form of fragrances: perfume oils. Over the last 75 years, Ajmal has carved a unique niche in the global fragrance industry as a fully integrated producer, manufacturer, distributor, and retailer. Offering a rich portfolio that blends traditional Arabian compositions with modern Western perfumery. Ajmal is especially renowned for its mastery in crafting the most exquisite Oud and Oud-related products. Today, Ajmal’s retail presence spans 400+ exclusive showrooms across the GCC and worldwide, with distribution in over 70 countries and a strong footprint across 80+ duty-free and travel retail points globally.
